title: Index Management on Microsoft SQL Server
breadcrumbs: Index Management on Microsoft SQL Server
text: Index Management on Microsoft SQL Server The following types of index are created: UNIQUE CLUSTERED—primary keys UNIQUE NON-CLUSTERED—candidate keys NON-UNIQUE, NON-CLUSTERED—all others The index is created when the table is created, either on-the-fly or by running SQL scripts generated by the Create Table facility. The syntax for index names is TableName I n , where n is the number of the index. If a table name is too long to allow this, within the 30 character limit, Uniface truncates it accordingly.
